scene.scenePrompt: Close-up, semi-transparent female silhouette in minimalist line art style, hovering in mid-air against a dark background, eyes staring directly at the viewer with an intense gaze, lips slightly parted as if speaking, abstract geometric lines and circles in emerald green to deep blue gradient radiating around her, faint green flame glow illuminating her translucent form from below, subtle hints of old bookstore setting out of focus: dim lamp light, rows of bookshelves fading into shadow, single floating page with charred edges, vertical composition, elegant abstract book poster layout, eerie and cold atmosphere.

beats.speaker: Lingge
beats.line: You're finally here... I've waited a hundred years.
beats.lineDelivery: Cold and resentful, trailing off at the end with a touch of etherealness

activeCharacters.name: Lingge
activeCharacters.pose: A translucent, illusory figure floating amidst eerie green flames, her long hair drifting without wind. She stares straight at you, a faint, uncanny smile playing on her lips.

beats.narration: Before the words can fully fade, the desk lamp flickers and dies completely. Darkness instantly swallows the bookstore, leaving only the faint, pulsing green glow from her body to illuminate your pale face.
beats.speaker: Lingge
beats.line: You released me, but you also awakened the curse. Now, only you can break it.
beats.lineDelivery: Her voice suddenly drops, laced with coercion and temptation, placing heavy emphasis on 'curse' and 'you'

activeCharacters.name: Lingge
activeCharacters.pose: Slowly drifting toward you, her translucent fingertips almost brushing your face, her eyes glowing faintly in the dark

choices.label: Ask for details about the curse
Reach out and close the book

effect.nextSceneSeed: You try to close the ancient tome, but the pages won't budge. Lingge's laughter echoes through the darkness.

beats.narration: Your throat tightens, your heart hammering like a drum.
beats.speaker: You
beats.line: What curse? Who on earth are you?

activeCharacters.name: Lingge
activeCharacters.pose: Withdrawing her fingers, she floats in mid-air, tilting her head to look at you like a curious child.

beats.speaker: Lingge
beats.line: I am the word spirit imprisoned within this book. A century ago, someone sealed me away using alchemy. Your actions just now tore a corner of that seal... but they also triggered the book's backlash.
beats.lineDelivery: Speaking faster, pausing abruptly at 'backlash' as her gaze sharpens

activeCharacters.name: Lingge
activeCharacters.pose: Crossing her arms over her chest, her expression turns solemn as the green glow around her ripples slightly.

choices.label: I'm willing to help free you

effect.nextSceneSeed: Lingge looks surprised and grateful, but then tells you that you need to find three other missing pages.
Lingge sneers, and the bookstore's shelves begin to shake violently, demonstrating the power of the curse.

characters.name: Lingge
characters.voiceDescription: Female, around 28 years old. Her voice is soft, cool, and slightly scholarly. She has an introverted yet firm personality, speaking at a moderate, rhythmic pace with occasional hints of mystery. Standard Mandarin, no distinct accent.
characters.visualDescription: female, mid-20s to early 30s, elegant and mysterious, deep blue hair with emerald green gradient, sleek bob cut, piercing green eyes with a contemplative gaze, fair complexion, sharp cheekbones, wearing a minimalist dark blue dress with geometric line-art patterns, accented by thin gold circles, elegant silver necklace with a crescent pendant, overall poetic and enigmatic aura, abstract geometric style.

storyState.logline: In a failing secondhand bookstore, you discover that cutting and pasting words can manifest real treasures—until you piece together a forbidden book and summon a word spirit imprisoned for a century. Her yearning for freedom and your obsession with saving the shop will unleash a textual storm that turns reality upside down.
storyState.protagonist: You are a clerk at a failing secondhand bookstore. In the midst of your desperation, you accidentally discover that cutting specific words from different books and pasting them into phrases turns them into real objects. You use this 'Text Alchemy' to save the bookstore's business, but while piecing together a forbidden book, you summon a word spirit who claims to have been imprisoned for a long time. She yearns for freedom, and you find yourself standing on the brink of losing control.
storyState.castNotes:
Lingge: A word spirit freed from the forbidden book. Though she appears pure and innocent, she harbors an extreme, deep-seated obsession with freedom, viewing you as the only key to breaking her seal. Your relationship is that of summoner and spirit, yet it also hints at unresolved past-life entanglements.
Auntie Chen: The true owner of the bookstore. A kind but stubborn elderly woman who treasures the bookstore as her life's blood. She knows nothing of your secret; she is your greatest motivation to save the shop, but also the weak spot that forces you to hide the truth.
storyState.synopsis: Late at night, you successfully use Text Alchemy in the secondhand bookstore to summon Lingge, a spirit from a forbidden book. She claims you have freed her but also awakened a curse. As darkness envelops the bookstore, Lingge demands that you help her break the seal, or the curse will fall upon you. You face a choice: trust her or doubt her.
openThreads: What exactly is the curse Lingge spoke of?
Why was she imprisoned for a century?
Is the price of Text Alchemy beginning to manifest?
relationships: Lingge: The summoned word spirit. She is both dependent on and wary of you, viewing you as the sole key to breaking her seal. Her cold demeanor is laced with probing curiosity.
storyState.nextHook: Next Scene: In the darkened bookstore, Lingge will either demonstrate the power of the curse or reveal the path to salvation. Your first choice will determine the direction of the story.

worldSetting:
This is an AI interactive story game for a [Female-oriented] audience, featuring intense dramatic conflicts and rapid twists in the style of short-form dramas.
Story Style: Multi-branching twists. Pacing: Fast-paced and satisfying.
Opening setup for the featured story 'Text Alchemy': You are a clerk at a failing secondhand bookstore who discovers that cutting and pasting specific word combinations from certain books turns them into real objects. You use this 'Text Alchemy' to save the bookstore, but while piecing together a forbidden book, you summon a 'word spirit' imprisoned within, who yearns for freedom.
Please start directly with this opening to give the player a strong sense of immersion and instant gratification; subsequent branches should maintain the twist density of short-form dramas, allowing players to see immediate consequences for every choice.
styleGuide: Abstract geometric book poster layout, vertical composition. An elegant, attractive female silhouette minimalist line-art seamlessly integrated into a vertical arrangement of intersecting lines, circles, and curves in a gradient of emerald green and deep blue. Dark background, elegant abstract design.
